2605.05931.title: In Data or Invisible: Toward a Better Digital Representation of Low-Resource Languages with Knowledge Graphs
2605.05931.paper_summary: Emerging digital technologies are exacerbating the existing divide in Open Access Data (OAD) between high-and low-resource languages, excluding many communities from participating in the global digital transformation. In this PhD proposal, we aim to address this gap, focusing on the language coverage of Linked Open Data knowledge graphs (LOD KGs). First, we identify key variables that characterize language distribution in LOD, including the number of Wikipedia articles per language edition and the number of language-tagged entities in LOD KGs. These variables are analyzed across three major multilingual LOD KGs, DBpedia, BabelNet, and Wikidata, providing insights into the representation and distribution of languages within LOD. Building on this analysis, we intend to study the impact of cross-lingual transfer candidate selection on the task of multilingual KG completion. In particular, we plan to investigate strategies based on linguistic proximity and the availability of curated annotated alignments between languages. Language proximity also motivates us to explore the benefits of analogical reasoning that relies on (dis)similarities and has not yet been investigated to identify correspondences across languages to improve KG completion performance and enhance language coverage in LOD.
2605.05931.paper_summary_zh: 新興的數位技術正在加劇高資源語言和低資源語言之間的開放存取數據（OAD）現有差距，使許多社群無法參與全球數位轉型。在這份博士提案中，我們旨在解決這一差距，專注於連結開放數據知識圖譜（LOD KGs）的語言覆蓋範圍。首先，我們確定了幾個關鍵變數，這些變數特徵化了LOD中的語言分佈，包括每種語言版本的維基百科文章數量和LOD KGs中語言標記實體的數量。這些變數在三個主要的多語言LOD KGs，即DBpedia、BabelNet和Wikidata中進行分析，提供了對LOD內語言表現和分佈的見解。基於這一分析，我們打算研究跨語言轉移候選選擇對多語言KG完成任務的影響。特別是，我們計畫調查基於語言接近性和語言之間經過策劃的註釋對齊的可用性所制定的策略。語言接近性也促使我們探索依賴於（不）相似性並尚未被研究的類比推理的好處，以識別語言之間的對應關係，從而改善KG完成性能並增強LOD中的語言覆蓋範圍。
2605.05931.author: Ndeye-Emilie Mbengue et.al.
